And again, it boils down to scale. Ultimately, as the Premier League, it is very challenging for us to constantly keep flying in coaches from abroad and scaling that. Doing individual sessions with kids alone can only give you that much. Whereas if you work on the coaches who are working locally with hundreds and thousands of kids, that gives you far more scale with a lot of impact.So, that is the reason why we fundamentally made a switch to focusing on coach development and grassroots because it allows us to do two things. One, we anyway want to work and uplift Indian coaches and the way they communicate and impart lessons and sessions to these kids and improve their standards. And secondly, it allows us to scale far better.So, we can have greater impact on hundreds and thousands of kids through the coaches.So, essentially, we work with partners, right? So, in this case, we are working with the OSCAR Foundation. So, the OSCAR programme has coaches and they recommend who are the right coaches who will benefit from these sorts of sessions.So, one is the local partnerships that we strike plus certain other partnerships that we have. Apart from the Next Gen Coach, we also have the Dream Sports Championship that we partnered with recently, or are supporting.So, in Dream Sports, essentially, our five elite coaches worked with the top youth academies at Premier League clubs. They came over here and had a lot of sessions with the top 16 academy coaches from India who had qualified for the DSE Championships in Goa, which is taking place as we speak.So, in a sense, they are self-selected because their teams qualified automatically. Those coaches have the opportunity to benefit from the Premier League coaches.So, this one currently is in Goa. What we are also looking to do is try and make it more inclusive. And we want to take this programme now to multiple cities. We had an activation in Mumbai in October.Now we’re doing this in Goa. And the plan is to obviously increase our geographic footprint in the country. But we need to do it in a responsible manner as well.So, we’re putting those plans in place as we speak to increase our footprint in India.So, I think scale is something that we’re looking at based on the objectives that we’re driving.Ultimately, over the last 18-20 years or so, we’ve had a positive impact on about 7,300 coaches, referees and educators, which has had a positive impact on 1,25,000 kids. Now, what we want to do is obviously try and improve that number.I think for a country like India, even 10,000 coaches or 15,000 coaches would still be small. How quickly can we get to that scale is something we have to think about. That’s why these are all attempts at fine-tuning our process, methodology and also seeing what the impact is for the market.If we’re seeing a lot of demand and coaches are generally benefiting from these sessions and workshops, then we obviously want to scale this very quickly.So, we don’t have a set number in mind to answer your question. I think it’s more dependent on how we are going to scale this, how effectively we can scale this, and what the impact is. So, the focus is on quality rather than quantity.The Premier League has been operating in India for the last 18 years. Across about 18 states, we’ve conducted activations at the grassroots level.What we want to do now, with the office here and having boots on the ground, with a team and everything in place, is obviously show up more consistently and more frequently, and take a hands-on approach through the season rather than shipping in and shipping out every six months or every year.That’s the fundamental objective. India is a key strategic market for the League itself.As part of that long-term commitment to the market, we want to bring our fans closer to the League as well. We want to make the League far more accessible to Indian fans.So, we work very closely with our broadcast partner to ensure the broadcast experience is seamless and find ways to activate in order to let our fans come and experience the pure joy, passion and tribalism of the Premier League.So, the fan engagement piece, fan development piece, is a big one for us in addition to grassroots.I would say the third leg to this whole thing is about football development. And that’s where strategic partnerships with key stakeholders in Indian football are going to be very fundamental.The first of which we already announced with the Dream Sports Championship.We want to genuinely play a role in uplifting Indian football and whatever help and support we can give to the Indian football ecosystem through the learnings and methodologies of the Premier League, we want to be able to share that.
Article Summary: English Premier League has launched a Next Gen Coach programme to support Indian coaches in their local communities. Building on its Community Coach Development Programme in Mumbai last year, the League is betting on a simple premise — that empowering one coach can create ripple effects across hundreds of young players.

Article Title: Will FIFA World Cup 2026 be broadcast in India? Here's the latest update
Article Text:
Image credit: X

NEW DELHI: Will the 2026 FIFA World Cup be broadcast in India? FIFA has now issued a fresh update amid uncertainty over whether fans in the country will be able to watch the tournament on television and digital streaming platforms.The 2026 FIFA World Cup, which will be jointly hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico, is scheduled to begin on June 11 next year. However, there is still no official broadcaster announced for India.In a statement shared with Times of India, FIFA confirmed that talks regarding the sale of media rights in India are still ongoing.“FIFA has concluded agreements with broadcasters in 180 territories around the world, ahead of the biggest ever FIFA World Cup. Discussions in India regarding sale of media rights for FIFA World Cup 2026 are ongoing and must remain confidential at this stage,” FIFA was quoted as saying.The issue has also reached the courts. The Delhi High Court on Tuesday issued notices to the Union Ministry of Information and Broadcasting and Prasar Bharati on a plea seeking the broadcast of the FIFA World Cup 2026 in India. The matter is expected to be heard again next week.The uncertainty over broadcasting rights is not limited to India. FIFA is yet to finalise a broadcast agreement in China as well, despite the tournament being just a month away. Reports earlier this week claimed that FIFA officials are expected to travel to Beijing to try and complete a deal.For Indian football fans, FIFA’s latest statement indicates that discussions are still active, although there is no confirmation yet on which broadcaster or streaming platform will telecast the tournament in the country.The 2026 edition will also be the biggest FIFA World Cup in history, featuring 48 teams for the first time.
Article Summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, which will be jointly hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico, is scheduled to begin on June 11 next year. However, there is still no official broadcaster announced for India. FIFA confirmed that talks regarding the sale of media rights in India are still ongoing.

Article Title: ‘I don’t normally like this’: Barcelona coach reacts to Lamine Yamal waving Palestine flag
Article Text:
FC Barcelona player Lamine Yamal holds a Palestinian flag (AP Photo)

FC Barcelona star Lamine Yamal once again found himself at the centre of attention during the club’s La Liga title celebrations after waving a Palestinian flag during the team’s open-top bus parade through the streets of Barcelona.The 18-year-old winger, regarded as one of football’s brightest young talents, was seen holding the Palestinian flag while celebrating Barcelona’s league triumph alongside teammates on Monday. Yamal later shared pictures of the moment on his Instagram account as thousands of supporters packed the streets to celebrate the title-winning campaign.According to reports, nearly 750,000 fans turned up for the celebrations as Barcelona marked another domestic triumph. Yamal’s gesture quickly drew global attention amid the ongoing humanitarian crisis in Gaza and the wider international reaction surrounding the conflict.Barcelona manager Hansi Flick was asked about the incident during a press conference on Tuesday. While the German coach admitted he generally prefers to avoid such situations, he also made it clear that Yamal is mature enough to make his own choices.“This I don’t normally like," Flick said. “I spoke with him.I said if he wants this, it is his decision. He is old enough. He’s 18 years old."There has been growing backlash across the world against Israel over the humanitarian impact of the war in Gaza, with demonstrations and protests appearing across several sports and cultural events, including football, cycling and basketball. Spain has also been among the countries protesting Israel’s inclusion in this year’s Eurovision Song Contest.Yamal, whose father is Moroccan and who is openly Muslim, appeared to express support for Palestinians through the gesture during the celebrations.Despite the controversy, Flick insisted his focus remained on the connection between the club and its supporters following the title-winning season.“We are playing football and you can see what the people expect from us," Flick added. "We are playing football to make the people happy. This is for me the first thing we have to do.”Yamal continues to rise rapidly in world football and is widely viewed as one of the leading candidates to become the sport’s next global superstar after Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo . The Spain international is also expected to be one of the standout names at the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal waved a Palestinian flag during Barcelona's La Liga title celebrations. The 18-year-old winger is regarded as one of football's brightest young talents. Yamal's gesture quickly drew global attention amid the ongoing humanitarian crisis in Gaza.

Article Title: When does the 2026 NFL season start? Who is Patrick Mahomes' Chiefs playing in week 1
Article Text:
When does the 2026 NFL season start (Paramount Plus)

When does the 2026 NFL season start and what defines Week 1 schedule?



What does the Kansas City Chiefs 2026 schedule reveal about their road ahead?



A new NFL season will begin in September with the league stretching its opening week across Wednesday to Monday, setting a different rhythm for fans. The Seattle Seahawks, the reigning Super Bowl champions, will host the New England Patriots on Sept. 9 in the NFL Kickoff Game. One day later, the Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers meet in Melbourne, Australia, for the league’s first regular-season game in the country. The Kansas City Chiefs open against the Denver Broncos as the full schedule brings fresh pressure and travel demands.Week 1 of the 2026 NFL season stretches from Wednesday, Sept. 9 to Monday, Sept. 14, breaking from the usual Sunday-only start. The Seahawks-Patiots matchup in Seattle opens the league’s 18-week grind, a rare Wednesday kickoff last seen in 2012 when scheduling avoided the Democratic National Convention.The following day shifts attention to Melbourne, where the Rams and 49ers meet in the NFL’s first regular-season game in Australia, also marking a broadcast partnership with Netflix. The rest of the slate returns to Sunday, with Monday Night Football closing the week.The season features a record nine international games across multiple continents.Kansas City enters 2026 after a 6-11 season that forced a reset across parts of the roster. The opening test comes against the Denver Broncos, a divisional game that sets an early tone. Much of the attention, however, stays on Patrick Mahomes, who tore his ACL on Dec. 14 against the Los Angeles Chargers. He is working through a roughly nine-month recovery window, and while he was seen throwing again in March, the team has avoided giving a firm return date. The Chiefs carry the fifth-hardest schedule in the league, shaped by AFC West battles and a demanding NFC West crossover. Road trips to the Bills, Seahawks, Bengals and Rams add weight, while Arrowhead will host the Patriots and 49ers. It is a difficult path, but the margin for error still hinges on Mahomes’ health and timing.The schedule release highlights how the league is leaning into global reach while keeping traditional rivalries intact. Early travel demands and short turnarounds will test depth across rosters, especially for teams balancing injuries and new coaching setups. For contenders like Kansas City, every stretch of the season carries added weight given last year’s downturn. For Seattle and San Francisco, the early international spotlight adds pressure but also opportunity. The opening week, spread across multiple days and continents, sets the tone for a season built on movement and adjustment rather than routine early.
Article Summary: The 2026 NFL season will begin on Wednesday, Sept. 9. The Seattle Seahawks host the New England Patriots in the NFL Kickoff Game. The Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers meet in Melbourne, Australia, for the league’s first regular- season game in the country. The Kansas City Chiefs open against the Denver Broncos.

Article Title: 2026 NFL schedule release: Netflix Christmas showcase features Chicago Bears, Denver Broncos, and more
Article Text:
2026 NFL schedule release: Netflix Christmas showcase features Chicago Bears, Denver Broncos, and more (Getty)

2026 NFL Schedule: Christmas showcase puts the Chicago Bears and Denver Broncos in the spotlight

The NFL made sure Christmas Day will again feel like a playoff weekend. The 2026 NFL schedule release confirmed three major holiday matchups featuring the Chicago Bears, Green Bay Packers, Buffalo Bills, Denver Broncos, Seattle Seahawks, and Los Angeles Rams.Netflix landed two of the games, while FOX will carry the final matchup later that night. More importantly, every game on the Christmas Day schedule already comes with fresh playoff history, divisional tension, or a revenge storyline attached.The Christmas schedule opens with one of the oldest rivalries in football as the Green Bay Packers travel to Chicago to face the Bears at 1 p.m. ET on Netflix. The matchup drew extra attention after Chicago eliminated Green Bay in the wild-card round at Soldier Field last season. That playoff loss changed the tone around the rivalry heading into 2026, especially with both teams expected to stay in the NFC playoff race.The second Netflix game features Buffalo visiting Denver at 4:30 p.m. ET, a rematch of last season’s AFC divisional-round thriller. The Denver Broncos won 33-30 in overtime, but the finish created heavy debate after officials ruled a late Brandin Cooks reception an interception following review.Bills fans spent months arguing about the decision, which now adds even more intensity to the rematch.The final Christmas game sends the Los Angeles Rams to Seattle for an NFC West showdown at 8:15 p.m. ET on FOX. The Seahawks beat Los Angeles in last season’s NFC Championship Game before eventually winning Super Bowl 60, giving Sean McVay’s team another major revenge opportunity later this season.The NFL also expanded Netflix’s role beyond Christmas. The streaming platform will air the Week 1 opener in Australia between the Rams and the San Francisco 49ers, the first-ever Thanksgiving Eve game between the Los Angeles Rams and the Green Bay Packers in Week 12, and one additional Week 18 matchup that the league will announce later in the season.A few years ago, the NFL treated Christmas as an occasional showcase. That approach changed quickly once holiday ratings exploded. Looking at the 2026 NFL schedule, the league now sees Christmas Day as another prime window for rivalry games, playoff rematches and national spotlight moments.
Article Summary: The 2026 NFL schedule will feature three major holiday matchups. The Chicago Bears, Green Bay Packers, Buffalo Bills, Denver Broncos, Seattle Seahawks, and Los Angeles Rams will all play. Netflix landed two of the games, while FOX will carry the final matchup.

Article Title: Chargers take subtle shot at Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy in viral 2026 NFL schedule release video
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini (Getty Images)

Why did the Chargers reference Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini in their schedule release video?



The Los Angeles Chargers turned one of the NFL’s messiest offseason storylines into the punchline of their 2026 schedule release video, and they did it without ever saying Mike Vrabel’s name. The franchise, long known for sharp social media humor, slipped multiple references to the New England Patriots coach’s ongoing controversy with former NFL reporter Dianna Russini into a Halo-themed release Thursday. With the Chargers set to face New England in Week 12, the video quickly became one of the most talked-about releases across the league, not because of the schedule itself, but because of how directly it leaned into a scandal the NFL has struggled to avoid.The joke landed within seconds for anyone following the story. During the Patriots segment, a sign reading “Next Photo Dump 1 Mile” flashes beside the road while a New York Post notification appears on screen. Both references point toward the photos and reports involving Vrabel and Russini that have circulated online over recent weeks.The Chargers never explained the joke outright. They did not need to. NFL fans connected the dots immediately, and social media did the rest.That reaction was expected inside league circles. The Chargers have built a reputation for aggressive and creative schedule release videos, often mixing gaming culture, internet humor and subtle shots at opponents. This year’s Halo-inspired concept gave the team another opening to push boundaries.What made this different was the target. Vrabel’s situation has remained one of the league’s most discussed offseason stories, especially after new photos and videos surfaced from his time with the Tennessee Titans. While the NFL has stayed publicly quiet, rival fan bases and media platforms have not shown the same restraint.The Chargers appeared to recognize that line had already shifted. Earlier this month, “Inside the NBA” used Vrabel and Russini in a “Gone Fishing” graphic, a moment that many around the league viewed as a sign the topic had become fair game for public humor.One source speaking to Front Office Sports said the Chargers were always the franchise most likely to test those limits.“The Chargers are masters at it. They don’t only take shots at teams; they take shots at individuals as well,” the source said.Still, there remains an understanding inside NFL circles that certain boundaries matter. Another source told the outlet, “Teams have to answer to one another. Coaches have to answer one another. People may have relationships with Vrabel, (Pats owner) Bob Kraft or (Pats executive vice president) Eliot Wolf. If you go there, people inside your own building may take issue with you. And if you dish it out, you better be able to take it. ”For now, the Chargers clearly decided the risk was worth it.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Chargers reference Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini in their schedule release video. The video quickly became one of the most talked-about releases across the league. The Chargers have built a reputation for aggressive and creative schedule release videos.

Article Title: Taylor Swift reportedly ditching traditional invitations to personally call guests for Travis Kelce wedding celebration
Article Text:
Taylor Swift reportedly making personal calls to invite celebrities for wedding (Getty Images)

What is Taylor Swift trying to protect with the secret wedding strategy?



Months after engagement rumors turned into a public announcement, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce appear to be tightening control around what could become one of the year’s most watched celebrity weddings. Instead of relying on printed invitations or formal save-the-dates, Swift is reportedly contacting guests herself by phone, according to TMZ. The move reflects how carefully the couple has handled their relationship since going public in 2023. Even with constant media attention surrounding them, both have managed to keep several personal milestones away from public view until they were ready to share them.The decision to avoid traditional invitations says a lot about the scale of attention surrounding the wedding. Printed save-the-dates often circulate online within hours, especially when A-list celebrities are involved. According to TMZ, Swift has chosen a more controlled approach, personally reaching out to some invitees while members of her team contact others. Guests are reportedly being told only that the wedding will happen sometime this summer, with exact details still being withheld.That level of secrecy may sound excessive for most couples, but it fits the pattern Swift and Kelce have followed since they began dating.Despite living under nonstop public scrutiny, they have kept large portions of their relationship remarkably private. Their engagement announcement in August 2025 came only after months of speculation, and since then, reports about the wedding location have shifted repeatedly.Early reports suggested the couple planned a Rhode Island ceremony. Later, Page Six reported New York City as the likely destination, with July 3 mentioned as a possible date. Another source claimed the pair considered changing venues altogether to accommodate a larger guest list. Conflicting reports have only added to the mystery, and some observers believe that may be intentional.The wedding itself is already shaping up to be a celebrity-heavy event. Reported members of Swift’s bridal party include Selena Gomez and Gigi Hadid, two of Swift’s closest longtime friends. That alone increases the pressure to keep details private until the last possible moment.Swift was also recently photographed in New York City wearing a white mini dress, something fans quickly connected to ongoing wedding speculation. Still, neither she nor Kelce has publicly confirmed any ceremony details. For now, the couple seems more focused on controlling the narrative than feeding public curiosity.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly planning a secret wedding this summer. Instead of relying on printed invitations or formal save-the-dates, Swift is reportedly contacting guests by phone. The move reflects how carefully the couple has handled their relationship since going public.

Article Title: “She Gave Me Over 20”: Ty Montgomery reveals how his mother turned one simple request into a life with over 20 Foster siblings
Article Text:
Ty Montgomery (Getty Images)

Ty Montgomery opens up about growing up in a home filled with more than 20 Foster siblings

What is Ty Montgomery doing after the NFL?

Ty Montgomery spent nine seasons in the NFL bouncing between six teams. Since retiring in 2024, he has been busier. The former running back sat down with PFN's Unguarded Access at Pro Athlete Community's Accelerate Event in Phoenix, a conference built to ease players into life after football. What came out was one of the more honest conversations Montgomery has had since hanging up his cleats, touching on family, faith, venture capital, and a children's book he wrote seven years ago.Ty Montgomery said the often-repeated figure never fully captured what his mother built at home. According to him, the number of foster siblings his family welcomed was actually more than 20.“My mom told me it’s really over 20,” Montgomery said of the sibling count. “But that just seemed like a number that was easy for everyone to wrap their brains around.”The story began when Montgomery was in third grade and asked his mother, Lisa Montgomery, for brothers. What started with one foster child named Lee eventually became a constantly growing household that changed the direction of his life.What stands out most is how strongly Montgomery rejects the traditional language attached to foster care.He does not describe those relationships as temporary or distant. To him, they remain permanent family ties.“Even though we don’t have a bloodline, we have a love line,” Montgomery said. “Their kids are my nieces and nephews, my kids are their nieces and nephews, their family is my family.”That experience now shapes much of his off-field work. Montgomery serves on the board of Connections Homes, a nonprofit focused on helping foster youth transition into adulthood with long-term mentoring families. He pointed to one detail in particular that matters deeply to him.“For the first time in their life, they get a choice on who they want to live with and who they want to be their family,” Montgomery said.One moment still stays with him. Montgomery helped fund a trip to Israel for former foster youth, many of whom had never flown before. A letter from one participant later changed his understanding of the impact.“She literally said in the letter that she was so grateful that this was happening because she didn’t think that she was worthy of something like this,” Montgomery said while trying not to get emotional. “I had no idea that’s the level of impact that I was making.”Football no longer defines Montgomery’s schedule, but his post-playing life appears even busier. He said his work now revolves around investing, faith, and writing.His venture firm, Next Legacy Partners, recently became one of the strategic investors tied to the NFL’s developing professional flag football league alongside TMRW Sports. Montgomery described the company’s interests as wide-ranging and constantly evolving.“One day it might be a voice AI company,” Montgomery said. “The next day it might be a flag football deal. The next day it might be a health tech company. The next day it might be defense tech company.”Faith, though, remains central to everything else. Montgomery spoke openly about how it grounded him through nine NFL seasons and continues to shape his decisions today.“Without my faith, I know I’d be a different man,” he said.He also confirmed he is in discussions with publishers about releasing a children’s book he wrote years ago. Looking back now, Montgomery believes the message was aimed at himself as much as future readers.
Article Summary: Ty Montgomery spent nine seasons in the NFL bouncing between six teams. Since retiring in 2024, he has been busier. His venture firm, Next Legacy Partners, recently became one of the strategic investors tied to the NFL’s developing professional flag football league.

Article Title: Marshawn Kneeland’s pregnant girlfriend Catalina Mancera shares painful update as grief continues months after Cowboys star’s death
Article Text:
Catalina Mancera and Marshawn Kneeland. (Instagram)

How has Catalina Mancera coped with Marshawn Kneeland’s death while preparing for motherhood?

Six months after the death of former Marshawn Kneeland, his girlfriend Catalina Mancera is preparing for a life-changing moment while still carrying the weight of an unimaginable loss. Mancera recently shared photos from her baby shower, surrounded by friends and family who have helped her through the months following Kneeland’s suicide in November. The emotional post offered a deeply personal glimpse into how those closest to the couple have rallied around her before the birth of their child, who is due in June. It also reopened a painful chapter that continues to linger around the Cowboys community and Kneeland’s family.Catalina Mancera posted an Instagram carousel from a baby shower held in Pilot Point, Texas, where she appeared in a white gown alongside relatives and close friends at Selah Event Venue. Decor at the event included cookies carrying messages such as “Baby Kneeland” and “A little piece of heaven is on the way,” a reminder that joy and grief are now tightly intertwined in her life.In the post, Mancera reflected on the support she has received since Kneeland’s death.“Words can’t explain how thankful I am for the people God has placed in my life,” Mancera wrote. “In such a hard season, the love and support surrounding me has made everything feel a little brighter. I’m so grateful for everyone who came out to celebrate me and baby, and even those who couldn’t make it but still showed love from afar.”“I also want to thank my family for throwing me such a beautiful shower in Michigan. I want to thank both of our families for all the love they’ve poured into me during this time. I’ll never forget the kindness, prayers, and support that have carried me through all of this.”ID@undefinedCaption not available.ID@undefinedCaption not available.The post quickly resonated with many followers because it showed the difficult balance Mancera has faced since November. Weeks after Kneeland’s death, she had already spoken openly about the emotional strain of preparing to become a mother without him beside her.“I dreamed of you long before I ever knew you. I dreamed of building a family, of the life your dad and I talked about and imagined together,” she wrote in December alongside sonogram photos. “I’m so excited for you, but my heart still aches.”Kneeland and Mancera met while he was playing at Western Michigan University. According to family members, their relationship began during his college football years before he eventually reached the NFL with the Dallas Cowboys.Authorities said Kneeland died by suicide at age 24 after a police pursuit in Frisco, Texas. Dispatch audio later revealed Mancera had contacted authorities after receiving alarming messages from him. Police also stated Kneeland had expressed suicidal thoughts before his death.
Article Summary: Marshawn Kneeland died by suicide in November. His girlfriend, Catalina Mancera, is expecting her first child in June. She recently shared photos from a baby shower held in Pilot Point, Texas. The post offered a deeply personal glimpse into how those closest to the couple have rallied around her.

Article Title: Dianna Russini controversy may cost Mike Vrabel millions, according to legal expert’s surprising new assessment
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel’s controversy with Dianna Russini takes dramatic turn after legal expert drops massive financial prediction (Getty Images)

Why Mike Vrabel could face a massive payout if Dianna Russini signs NDA after controversy

The Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy is far from settling. Weeks after the alleged affair story surfaced in April 2026, fresh claims keep emerging. The latest involves a possible non-disclosure agreement, with Russini reportedly in discussions to sign one following her departure from The Athletic. Legal analyst Mike McCann appeared on WEEI to explain what such a deal would actually mean, and more importantly, who should be footing the bill.During a recent appearance on WEEI, sports law expert Mike McCann explained why any agreement could place the financial burden directly on Mike Vrabel rather than the Patriots organization. McCann was direct about the enforceability of an NDA."That would be a non-disclosure agreement; it can be enforceable. She is an adult. If she signs some sort of agreement saying that I will not disclose other materials about you, whatever they did, that's a contract," he said.But the more pointed question he raised was about the Patriots' involvement. If this is a personal matter involving Vrabel, why would the organisation be tied to any financial arrangement? "Why are the Patriots paying for it? Why wouldn't it be Vrabel? He is the one at the issue," McCann said.It's a fair point. Russini resigned from The Athletic after the story generated sustained public attention. She has not publicly said what her next step is. McCann suggested that given the professional consequences she has already faced, she could reasonably push for a significant payout if an NDA does materialise.The complication, however, is that not everything connected to this story can be legally contained. Third-party material, like old photos and videos that surfaced publicly, would fall entirely outside the scope of any agreement. McCann was candid about this limitation:"I don't know if it's also a good idea, I mean, in a way, some of these disclosures aren't even from them. How about that photo from six years ago at a bar, you can't buy silence of the world. So there's other people that have stuff that wouldn't be covered by the agreement."The New England Patriots, for their part, have reportedly shown no inclination to part ways with Vrabel as head coach. But the legal and public relations dimensions of this story are still very much in motion. Whether a formal NDA gets signed, and who pays for it, remains an open question.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini is reportedly in discussions to sign a non-disclosure agreement. Sports law expert Mike McCann says the financial burden would be on Mike Vrabel. McCann also questioned why the Patriots would be tied to any financial arrangement.

Article Title: Fact check: Did Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce secretly plan an Indian-inspired wedding ceremony?
Article Text:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumours spark Rishi Patel frenzy

Who is Rishi Patel and why fans are obsessed with the Taylor Swift wedding reports?

Wedding rumours surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have taken an unexpected international turn after fresh speculation linked the couple to one of the world’s top event designers. Social media exploded this week after reports claimed the pair may be planning a wedding with a possible Indian-inspired touch.The buzz started after online rumours suggested celebrated designer Rishi Patel could be involved in organising the highly anticipated ceremony. While no official confirmation has been made by Swift or Kelce’s teams, fans immediately began imagining what could become one of the most talked-about celebrity weddings in recent memory.The online reaction was instant. Fans across X quickly turned the unverified reports into a trending discussion filled with excitement, predictions, and wedding theories.One fan wrote, "IT'S GOING TO BE HUGE! Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have reportedly hired Rishi Patel as their wedding planner, one of the world's biggest designers."Another post read, "IT WILL BE THE MOST BEAUTIFUL EVENT! Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly hired Rishi Patel as their wedding planner, named as one of the world's top designers by multiple magazines.”Others pushed the hype even further. “Oh, this wedding is about to be in the history books, hitting the person that styled Kate Middleton and Rishi Patel," one user posted.Another simply called the possible event “generational.”A separate fan added, "I just know this wedding is gonna be extra and we love her for that."The rumours gained traction partly because Patel’s work already carries a global reputation for luxury and dramatic visual storytelling. If true, the wedding could blend high-fashion Western glamour with elegant South Asian-inspired aesthetics, something fans are already obsessing over online.Rishi Patel serves as CEO and Chief Creative Officer of HMR Designs, a Chicago-based event production company known for extravagant celebrity and corporate events. Over the years, Patel has earned recognition from Vogue, Harper’s Bazaar, Brides, and Martha Stewart Weddings for his large-scale wedding concepts and luxury event styling.His client list alone explains why Swift fans became instantly invested in the rumours. Patel and his company have reportedly worked with President Obama, Chanel, Google, and even the NFL.That NFL connection only fueled speculation further because of Kelce’s deep ties to the league.For now, there is still no official wedding confirmation from Swift or Kelce. But with engagement rumours already dominating entertainment headlines for months, the possibility of an internationally inspired celebrity wedding has added a completely new layer to the frenzy surrounding the superstar couple.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumours spark Rishi Patel frenzy. Social media exploded this week after reports claimed the pair may be planning a wedding with a possible Indian-inspired touch. Patel serves as CEO and Chief Creative Officer of HMR Designs, a Chicago-based event production company.

Article Title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni’s settlement may not end the drama for Taylor Swift ahead of her wedding
Article Text:
(Getty Images)

Taylor Swift and Blake Lively's friendship drama intensifies after Justin Baldoni case settlement



The Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni saga is technically over. Except it really isn't. And with Taylor Swift's wedding reportedly weeks away, things are about to get very complicated, very fast.The settlement was reached about two weeks before jury selection was set to begin on May 18. No trial. No jury. No dramatic courtroom showdown. A judge had already tossed ten of Lively's thirteen claims — including sexual harassment and defamation, leaving just three remaining: retaliation, aiding and abetting retaliation, and breach of contract. None of those were against Baldoni personally, but against his production company and a PR firm.Settlement terms haven't been confirmed, and claims that Lively walked away with no cash are already circulating. Baldoni's lawyer, for his part, said his client was "ecstatic." Make of that what you will.This is where it gets messy. The Baldoni case dragged Taylor Swift into it whether she wanted to be or not. Private texts between Swift and Lively were revealed in court documents, and insiders say Taylor felt "exposed and violated," and that it crossed a line.The fallout has been building for months. Sources say Taylor "no longer trusts Blake or Ryan" and wants a "drama-free day." One insider was blunt: "They are not invited. Period."Despite everything, Blake is reportedly convinced the friendship can be saved and has already picked out a dress for Taylor's wedding. Sources say she "sees herself as inner circle not optional, essential," pointing to the fact that she was around when Taylor and Travis first got together.Bold. Very bold.With Taylor and Travis's big day reportedly approaching, most reports point to July 3 in New York, the guest list question isn't going away. Blake wasn't included in the reported bachelorette party planning, with Selena Gomez, Gigi Hadid, the Haim sisters, Brittany Mahomes, and Kylie Kelce reportedly on that list instead.If you're not on the bachelorette list, the wedding odds aren't looking great.The case settled. The chaos didn't. Blake Lively is out here dress shopping for a wedding she may not be invited to, while Taylor Swift is reportedly trying to keep her big day completely drama-free.Travis Kelce is just trying to get married. Honestly, same.
Article Summary: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni settled out of court. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly getting married on July 3. Sources say Taylor "no longer trusts Blake or Ryan" and wants a "drama-free day" Despite everything, Blake is reportedly convinced the friendship can be saved.

Article Title: Who is Rishi Patel? The man allegedly hired for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding
Article Text:
(Image via Backgrid)

Meet the celebrity designer, Rishi Patel, reportedly hired for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding



Reports claim Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are planning an over-the-top luxury wedding in New York



The Tayvis wedding just got a major upgrade. Allegedly. Word dropped overnight that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have brought in celebrity event designer Rishi Patel to plan their big day. And if that name means nothing to you, it absolutely should. Rishi Patel is known for his grandeur and aesthetics.Not just some wedding guy with a Pinterest board. Patel is the CEO and Chief Creative Officer of HMR Designs, a firm with over 40 years of experience, and has been named one of the top wedding and event designers in the world by Harper's Bazaar, Vogue, Brides, and Martha Stewart Weddings. He's also a Top 40 Under 40 honouree. His whole thing is transformative, over-the-top luxury events for the world's most notable families and figures.HMR Designs previously handled decor for Jennifer Gates and Nayel Nassar's wedding. So yeah. This is the guy you call when the wedding needs to be a whole moment.DeuxMoi was the source of this rumor, saying, "Word on the wedding planner street," claiming Patel had been brought on to design the couple's special day. No confirmation from Swift, Kelce, or Patel himself. But when has that ever stopped anyone from going absolutely feral online? Quite a bit is floating around. Reports point to a July 3 wedding in New York, an indoor venue, and guests already under strict NDAs.Tight operation. Very Taylor. Industry insiders are apparently calling this one "generational." Bold claim. But also... not hard to believe.The expected guest list includes Patrick and Brittany Mahomes, Jason Kelce as likely best man, Donna Kelce, Taylor's childhood BFF Abigail Anderson, and close collaborator Jack Antonoff. Basically, if you're not on the list, you're watching the recap on Instagram like the rest of us.Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are doing what they always do, letting just enough leak to keep the whole world talking, while confirming absolutely nothing. Classic.But bringing in Rishi Patel, if true, tells you everything. This isn't going to be a low-key backyard situation. It's going to be a production. An era. Probably the most talked-about celebrity wedding in years.July, honestly, cannot come fast enough.
Article Summary: Celebrity event designer Rishi Patel reportedly hired for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. Patel is the CEO and Chief Creative Officer of HMR Designs. Reports point to a July 3 wedding in New York, an indoor venue, and guests already under strict NDAs.
